functions.name: microsoft_calendar.get_all_available_slots
functions.description: Retrieve all available meeting time slots across multiple attendees using Microsoft Graph getSchedule (15-minute free/busy).

args.days_to_check: optional integer (default: 3, number of days to scan)

returns.slots: list of dicts with { start, end, startDate }
returns.return_str: string (human-readable summary of available time slots)

functions.name: microsoft_calendar.create_calendar_event
functions.description: Create a Microsoft Outlook calendar event with optional Microsoft Teams meeting.

returns.hangoutLink: string (Teams join URL if online meeting)
returns.return_str: string (e.g. 'Meeting "Demo Call" created on 22 Sep 2025 from 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM with attendees: a@x.com, b@y.com. Microsoft Teams link added.')

functions.name: microsoft_calendar.update_calendar_event
functions.description: Update an existing Microsoft Outlook calendar event, including Teams meeting enable/disable.

args.title: optional string
args.attendees: optional list of attendee email addresses
args.googlemeet: boolean (true = enable Teams, false = disable Teams)

returns.return_str: string (e.g. 'Meeting "Demo Call" was updated and is now scheduled on 23 Sep 2025 from 11:00 AM – 11:30 AM. Teams meeting enabled.')

functions.name: microsoft_calendar.delete_calendar_event
functions.description: Delete a Microsoft Outlook calendar event.

returns.return_str: string (e.g. 'The calendar event has been successfully deleted.')

functions.name: microsoft_service.send_email
functions.description: Send an email using Microsoft Graph. Automatically detects and sends Text or HTML body.

returns.return_str: string (e.g. 'Email with subject "Meeting Confirmation" was sent to user@example.com using HTML format.')
